affordances.name: Commitment evidence revision check
affordances.mechanism: When a public, costly, or identity-linked commitment is being defended against contrary evidence, require the answer to separate evidence updating from comfort-restoring belief revision and add pre-set exit or review criteria.

use_when: A person or team is defending a prior commitment after contrary signals appear.
The commitment is public, ego-identified, costly, or hard to reverse.
Evidence avoidance, rationalization, or escalation may be preserving psychological consistency.
do_not_use_when: The discomfort is simply a useful sign of unresolved uncertainty and no commitment defense is visible.
The case lacks evidence of prior commitment, identity threat, or contrary signals.
The active risk is private dissent being pulled into public team consensus before independent assessment.
A better fit is purely financial sunk-cost analysis or operational bottleneck diagnosis.
case_evidence_needed: The prior commitment and who is identified with it.
The contrary signal being minimized or avoided.
The current rationalization or escalation behavior.
Any kill criteria, leading indicators, or independent review path.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to name the discomfort-preserving commitment, the evidence being defended against, and a review mechanism that makes changing course socially and psychologically cheaper.
evidence_required: Predefined criteria for continuing, changing, or stopping.
good_output_shape: Distinguishes honest belief updating from comfort-restoring rationalization.
Asks what evidence would make the prior commitment wrong.
Adds kill criteria or independent review before more commitment.
Protects public mind-changing as good judgment, not humiliation.

diagnostic_questions: What commitment would become uncomfortable if this evidence were taken seriously?
Are we revising beliefs toward accuracy or toward consistency with what we already did?
What would we do if this same project or position were brought fresh today?
misuse_guards: Do not treat all discomfort as cognitive dissonance.
Do not use the label to psychoanalyze motives without commitment and evidence-avoidance signals.
Do not resolve the tension by urging consistency; the source frames the risk as motivated belief revision.

source_evidence.source_quote: people do not change behavior to align with beliefs nearly as often as they change beliefs to align with behavior
a public, ego-identified commitment has been made
Apply explicit kill criteria before committing resources

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 14 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports dissonance as post-commitment belief revision requiring exit criteria and independent review.
dropped_material: Did not extract generic discomfort management.
Did not convert cognitive dissonance into motive certainty.
open_questions: Later packet review should test overlap with commitment-bias and sunk-cost records.

affordances.name: Private doubt before group consensus
affordances.mechanism: When visible leadership commitment or public team consensus creates pressure for members to revise private doubts toward the group position, require independent anonymous assessment before discussion and protect dissent from social consequences.

use_when: A leader or group has visibly committed to a position before members give independent assessments.
Individual members may have private doubts that conflict with the public team consensus.
Social pressure may cause people to revise private beliefs toward the team position.
The case needs dissent captured before group discussion hardens the consensus.
do_not_use_when: The case is an individual post-commitment rationalization with no group-consensus pressure.
Independent anonymous assessments have already been collected and reviewed before discussion.
The disagreement is already public, protected, and evidence-linked.
The output would use anonymous feedback to avoid accountability rather than surface dissonance pressure.
case_evidence_needed: The leader commitment or public team consensus shaping the room.
The private doubts, minority arguments, or devil's-advocate concerns at risk of suppression.
The assessment method used before group discussion.
The protection against social consequences for raising the dissent.
How the group will engage the most opposed argument after independent assessment.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to separate evaluation from commitment by collecting independent anonymous assessments before discussion and protecting the dissenting argument from social punishment.
evidence_required: The visible commitment or consensus pressure.
The private doubt or opposed argument being suppressed.
The social consequence or status pressure that would silence dissent.
good_output_shape: Names the public consensus or leader commitment.
Captures private assessments before group discussion.
Preserves the strongest opposed argument for real engagement.
Protects devil's advocates from social consequences.

diagnostic_questions: What private doubt would become harder to voice after the group discusses this openly?
Have independent, anonymous assessments been collected before consensus pressure starts?
What would the person most opposed to this plan say, and have we genuinely engaged with that argument?
What protection keeps devil's advocates from paying a social price for raising the concern?
misuse_guards: Do not use this card to hide responsibility for a critique that can be safely raised in the open.
Do not treat all team disagreement as cognitive dissonance; look for public consensus pressure against private doubt.
Do not substitute anonymous polling for genuine engagement with the strongest opposing argument.
Do not merge this with sunk-cost exit criteria when the live failure mode is social belief revision before discussion.

source_evidence.source_quote: individual members face personal dissonance between their private doubts and the public team consensus
Social pressure amplifies the individual drive to revise private beliefs toward the team position.
Require independent, anonymous assessments before group discussion.
Protect devil's advocates from social consequences.

review_notes.normalization_note: PR86 split group dissonance from individual commitment revision because the receiver action is different: capture anonymous private assessment before discussion and protect dissent from social consequences.
dropped_material: Did not extract generic groupthink; this card activates specifically when public consensus creates dissonance with private doubts.
Did not replace kill-criteria review for individual or project commitment defense.
open_questions: Packet stress review should test whether this card routes separately from peer-review-your-perspectives and psychological-safety.

absence_records.reason: The source treats dissonance as a drive toward motivated resolution, not proof that the uncomfortable belief or action is wrong.

source_evidence.source_quote: motivated belief revision, not a mechanism for honest belief updating

absence_records.reason: The source supports belief revision through commitments and behavior; it does not support claiming a private attitude changed without evidence of the commitment context.

source_evidence.source_quote: The public advocacy creates a cognition

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 14 source reading found strong reviewed depth for cognitive dissonance as a commitment-review and evidence-avoidance guard.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, deterministic pressure, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet review should decide when this card or commitment-bias should carry the sharper treatment.
